note: Infrastructure provisioning is out of scope for v1 product design. This document captures decisions so infrastructure teams can provision and deploy independently.

frontend.rationale: Full-stack capable, excellent Microsoft Entra ID integration via MSAL, strong ecosystem, easy to staff.

backend.rationale: Single codebase and deployment for v1 simplicity. Can be separated into a standalone API in a future release if needed.

database.rationale: Open source, fully managed on Azure, well-suited to structured relational inventory data.

authentication.rationale: Recommended for states operating a Microsoft tenant — agencies already authenticate here, so no new identity system is required. The authentication layer can be replaced with any SAML 2.0 or OIDC-compatible identity provider for states that do not use Microsoft Entra ID.
authentication.alternativeNote: Non-Microsoft-tenant states may substitute any SAML 2.0 or OIDC-compatible identity provider (e.g., Okta, PingFederate, Auth0). Replace MSAL with the appropriate library for your chosen provider.

hosting.rationale: Entra ID lives in Azure. Keeping hosting in the same tenant simplifies security, compliance, and networking. States using a different identity provider may host on any compatible platform.

notifications.rationale: Email notifications within the same Azure tenant. No third-party dependency. Can be replaced with any transactional email service if not using Azure.

styling.rationale: Fast to build with, easy to maintain, no heavyweight design system required for v1.

containerization.strategy: Optional — not a mandate. Code-first deployment always available.

developerExperience.rationale: Single command spins up app and database locally. Recommended developer onboarding path.

deploymentPaths.description: Deploy directly to any platform without containers. Any state can use this path regardless of container expertise.
Build and run as a Docker container. Portable across any cloud or on-premise environment.
deploymentPaths.audience: States that want portability and environment consistency

deploymentPaths.description: Deploy to a Kubernetes cluster for states with existing container infrastructure.
deploymentPaths.audience: Larger states with container infrastructure already in place

infrastructureAssumptions.azureTenant: State IT authority Azure tenant — to be confirmed by infrastructure team
infrastructureAssumptions.azureSubscription: To be provisioned by infrastructure team
infrastructureAssumptions.identityProviderGroups: Agency groups and role assignments to be configured in the state identity provider tenant
infrastructureAssumptions.provisioningStatus: Not started — see this file for stack decisions to guide provisioning
